question: I am unable to make part payment, please guide.
answer:
There are many reasons for this -

- There is an overdue amount on your loan account that needs to be cleared

- If there is a last EPI (EQUATED PERIODIC INSTALMENT (EPI)) pending. In this case, proceed directly for Foreclosure

- The part pre-payment amount exceeds the NEFT limit pre-defined by your bank. In this case, please check with your bank

- If there are network issues. Please wait for some time before re-trying

question: Does Bajaj Finance Limited consider my CIBIL score for every loan I take?
answer: Yes. Bajaj Finance Limited considers the CIBIL score for your every loan.

question: Does checking the CIBIL report repeatedly hamper my CIBIL score?
answer: When you check your CIBIL score, it is considered a ‘soft inquiry’ and doesn’t make any negative impact on your credit score. However, if a lender or a credit card issuer requests CIBIL to check your credit report (usually when they are considering you for a loan or credit card), it is counted as a ‘hard inquiry’. Hard inquiries are recorded in the enquiry section of your credit report. Multiple hard inquiries, if done over a relatively short period, demonstrate what is called ‘credit hungry behaviour’ and may hurt your credit score. But a soft inquiry, at least once a year, is considered an important financial habit.

question: How can I improve my CIBIL score?
answer:
If you have a poor CIBIL score, there is plenty you can do to improve it. Here are some handy tips:

- Avoid being a co-applicant for a loan unless you are sure of not planning to borrow anytime soon

- Ensure you repay all your EMIs and credit card bills on time

- Be cautious when borrowing and always have a proper repayment plan in place

question: How can I maintain a good CIBIL score?
answer:
Here are a few simple tips to maintain a good CIBIL score:

- Pay your monthly instalments on time to build a good track record

- Manage your credit card carefully, set payment reminders and limit your usage

- Choose longer loan tenure carefully, try to make part-prepayments when you can

question: How do I read my CIBIL TransUnion Report (CTR)?
answer:
CIBIL TransUnion Report contains detailed information on the credit you have availed such as home loans, credit cards, personal loans, automobile loans and overdraft facilities. It is divided into 6 parts:

- CIBIL Score

- Personal Information

- Contact Information

- Employment Information

- Account Information

- Enquiry Information

question: What do you mean by CTR?
answer: CIBIL TransUnion Report is a report of your CIBIL score which indicates how well or how poorly you have dealt with your loans or credit cards in the past. It includes detailed information on all your past/current loans and their EMIs. Also, timely repayment of loans helps you maintain a healthy CIBIL score.

question: What do you mean by DPD?
answer: DPD stands for Days Past Dues and tells you about the days for which a payment has been delayed.

question: What is my CIBIL score and how does it work?
answer:
CIBIL score is a three-digit number, ranging from 300 to 900, that represents your creditworthiness. This score is derived after taking into consideration the credit history and details found in your CIBIL report, which is maintained as a record by TransUnion CIBIL.

Your lender checks your CIBIL score, before approving a loan, to verify your ability to repay the loan. The closer you are to a credit score of 900, the better are your chances of getting a loan. A score closer to 300 is considered poor and lowers your possibility of getting easy approvals.

question: Why do lenders check my CIBIL score before approving a loan?
answer:
Your CIBIL score measures your creditworthiness. Lenders choose to check your score for several reasons. Some of the most important ones include:

 - Checking credit record and history

 - Measuring your capacity to repay the loan

 - Reviewing credit balance and sensing the risk level of your profile

 - Identifying if you meet the lender’s loan eligibility criteria

 - Arriving at the loan amount and interest rate suitable for you

question: Why is my loan status not reported to CIBIL?
answer:
This may be because of the following reasons:

- CIBIL update for your loan status is sent to CIBIL after 2 days of the loan closure

- EPI (EQUATED PERIODIC INSTALMENT (EPI)) status for your loan is sent to CIBIL after the month ends

question: How can I cancel my policy?
answer:
You can cancel your insurance policy/ membership if it is within Free Look Period (FLP). Free Look Period (FLP) is a trial period that starts on the day your insurance policy/ membership is issued and allows you to assess the policy/ membership and its benefits before deciding whether to continue or cancel the same.

To cancel your insurance policy/ membership, follow the steps below -

- Click on 'My Account' at the bottom right of the screen

- Click on ‘My Relations’

- Click on 'Insurance/ Value Added Services' tab at the top of the page

- Click on arrow button on relationship of your choice

- Click on ‘Cancel Policy/ membership’

To cancel now, please click here.

question: Will my CIBIL score get impacted if I foreclose a loan?
answer: Not at all. Once you foreclose a loan, the same will be reported to CIBIL as 'Closed' along with '0 (zero) Outstanding'.

question: When can I foreclose my loan?
answer: You can foreclose a loan any time after clearing the 1st EPI (EQUATED PERIODIC INSTALMENT (EPI)).

question: What should I do if my payment through UPI doesn't get updated against my loan?
answer:
This may happen due to:

1. Some issues with the payment gateway or

2. Poor network connectivity

Moreover, if your transaction at our end fails due to any of the above-given reasons, then the amount debited gets refunded to your bank account.

To know about the refund timeframe, please connect with your bank.

question: Is it necessary for me to do E-Mandate registration?
answer:
Yes, this is mandatory if you have availed loan or opted for Systematic Deposit Plan from us. You can either do the registration online or offline. Mandate/NACH registration allows for easy auto-debiting of payments/ EMIs/ investments from your registered bank account basis the agreed frequency.

If you prefer doing this offline, visit the branch closest to you and our representatives will help you.

question: What do you mean by E-Mandate/ E-NACH?
answer: This is an online, one-time registration process of setting up a payment instruction to debit your verified bank account. In other words, you authorize BFL (Bajaj Finance Ltd.) to debit your bank account towards EMIs/investment amounts automatically.

question: What do you mean by NACH?
answer: As per NPCI website the definition states 'The National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) offers to banks, financial institutions, Corporates and Government/s a service termed as “National Automated Clearing House (NACH)” which includes both Debit and Credit. It shall be referred to as NACH. NACH (Debit) & NACH (Credit) aims at facilitating interbank high volume, low value debit/credit transactions, which are repetitive in nature, electronically using the NPCI service.
